content.problemStatement:
Chefland consists of three countries named A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC.


Country AAA exports goods worth A1A_1A1​ units and imports goods worth A2A_2A2​ units.
Country BBB exports goods worth B1B_1B1​ units and imports goods worth B2B_2B2​ units.
A trade surplus occurs when a country exports **strictly more** than it imports.

Find whether country CCC is in trade surplus.

Note that the countries A,B,CA,B,CA,B,C trade only between themselves.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of four space-separated integers A1,A2,B1A_1, A_2, B_1A1​,A2​,B1​ and B2B_2B2​ — denoting the exports and imports of countries AAA and BBB respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES`, if country CCC is in trade surplus and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers A1, A2, B1, B2 — the export and import values of countries A and B respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if country C is in trade surplus, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: (5-3)+(2-4) = 2-2 = 0, not negative, so C is not in surplus.
(2-5)+(3-1) = -3+2 = -1 < 0, therefore C has a trade surplus.

solution.approach: For each test case compute net export of A as A1 - A2 and net export of B as B1 - B2. The net export of C must balance the total, therefore netC = -(netA + netB). Country C has a trade surplus iff netC > 0, i.e., (A1 - A2) + (B1 - B2) < 0. So simply check this inequality and print YES or NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
